generated_body:
Hi Bryan,

I know this likely came from an automated sequence — figured a genuine reply was worth it anyway.

One thing that stood out in onboarding: the default-branch + role + connection-string flow at first DB provisioning could use a more opinionated default. Coming from RDS or Supabase, the mental model shift on Neon's branching takes a beat, and that's probably the first place someone drops off.

Separately — Brad Van Vugt's credits email (Agent Platform Program) landed two days after yours. We're building agent infra at Zerg with per-tenant DBs, which looks like exactly that program's profile. Is Brad the right routing there, or does that come through you? Just want to make sure it lands in the right place.

Open to a chat product-to-product if that'd be useful.

Best,
Matthew

Hi Underdog team,

I'm Matt, one of the founders at Zerg (zergai.com) — we're an early-stage AI startup, and I'm putting together our first internal team swag run. Shortlisted Underdog specifically for the premium streetwear feel; we'd rather spend a bit more for hoodies that punch above a standard blank.

Here's what we're looking at:

- T-shirts: ~80 units
- Embroidered hats: ~40 units
- Hoodies: ~20 units

Quantities are somewhat flexible — happy to bump if there's a meaningful price break at a higher tier.

Designs will be supplied production-ready (single-color or small-color logo, straightforward placement). Would love to get back from you:

- Garment recommendations: your best soft-feel tee, hoodie blank, and hat for embroidery
- Per-unit pricing at the quantities above, plus any volume-break tiers worth knowing about
- Turnaround time
- Sample/proof policy

Once we've confirmed fit, happy to send art files over. Let me know if there's a better intake path on your end.

Best,
Matthew

Hi Real Thread team,

Hope all is well!

I'm Matt Eisner — I run Zerg (zergai.com), an early-stage AI startup. We're putting together our first internal team swag run and shortlisted Real Thread based on your soft tri-blend reputation.

Here's what we're looking at:

- T-shirts: ~80 units
- Embroidered hats: ~40 units
- Hoodies: ~20 units

Quantities are a bit flexible — if there's a meaningful price break at a higher tier, happy to hear about it.

Designs will be production-ready (logo + placement specs, single-color or small-color). Premium feel matters more than rock-bottom price for us.

Would appreciate a quote covering:

- Garment recommendations (your best soft-feel tee, hoodie blank, and hat for embroidery)
- Per-unit pricing at those quantities, plus any volume-break tiers
- Turnaround time
- Sample/proof policy

Happy to send art files once we've confirmed a good fit. Let me know if you need anything else from me to put together a quote.

Best,
Matthew

Hi,

I'm Matt — founder at Zerg (zergai.com), an early-stage AI company. I'm putting together a first run of internal team swag and shortlisted Threadbird based on your screen-print and embroidery reputation with tech and creative brands.

Here's what I'm looking at:

- T-shirts: ~80 units
- Embroidered hats: ~40 units
- Hoodies: ~20 units

Quantities are somewhat flexible — happy to bump if there's a meaningful price break at a higher tier.

Designs will be supplied production-ready (single-color or small-color logo, standard placement).

Could you send over:

- Garment recommendations for each (soft-feel tee, hoodie blank, hat for embroidery)
- Per-unit pricing at the quantities above, plus any volume-break tiers worth flagging
- Turnaround time
- Sample/proof policy

Premium feel matters more than lowest price for us. Happy to send art files once we confirm fit.

Best,
Matthew

Looking for a quote on a die-cut vinyl sticker run for Zerg AI — three designs at ~3", split evenly, somewhere in the 500–1000 range. Can you send pricing at the 500/750/1000 tiers?

Also curious whether holographic/mirror stock is worth it for laptop stickers, whether you do physical sample packs, and rough lead time. Vector art is ready for two of the three.

Thanks,
Matt

Looking for a quote on a die-cut vinyl sticker run for Zerg AI — three ~3" designs split evenly, 500–1000 total. Can you send pricing at the 500/750/1000 tiers, plus lead time and whether holographic/mirror stock is worth it for laptop stickers? Also — do you do physical sample packs? Vector art's ready for two of three; shipping to Michigan or SF, TBD.

Thanks,
Matt

Hi there — looking to print 500–1000 stickers across 3 designs for Zerg, two of which are graphic-heavy dev-culture pieces I think suit silkscreen well. Could you send pricing tiers plus your take on silkscreen vs digital for bold graphics? Also curious about sample pack availability and lead time. Vector art's ready for two of the three, and we'd ship to Michigan or SF (still TBD).

Thanks!
Matt

Looking for a quote on a die-cut vinyl sticker run for Zerg AI - three ~3" designs (our logo plus two dev-culture graphics), split evenly, somewhere in the 500-1000 range. Could you send pricing at the 500/750/1000 tiers, plus lead time?

Also curious whether premium stock (holographic/mirror) is worth it for laptop stickers, and whether you do physical sample packs. Vector art's ready for two of the three; shipping to Michigan or SF, TBD.

Thanks,

Matthew

Hi Sticker Robot team,

I'm putting together a first sticker run for Zerg (zergai.com) - 500-1000 units across 3 designs, two of which are graphic-heavy dev-culture pieces I think suit silkscreen well. Premium feel matters more to us than lowest price.

Could you send pricing tiers at those quantities, plus your take on silkscreen vs digital for bold graphics? Sample pack availability and lead time would help too.

Vector art's ready for two of the three, and we'd ship to Michigan or SF (still TBD). Happy to send files once we confirm fit.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Scout Books team,

We're putting together a first run of custom hand-bound pocket notebooks for our team and community at Zerg (zergai.com) - 50-100 units, our logo foil-stamped or debossed on the cover. Premium feel matters more than lowest price for us.

Could you send pricing across that range, with your take on foil vs deboss and cover material options? Curious about samples and lead time too - shipping to either Michigan or SF, still TBD.

Happy to send art files once we confirm fit.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Anila,

Thanks for this. A couple of corrections - our stickers are ~3 inches on the longest side rather than 2x2, and we'd like to be invoiced in USD.

I've attached the art: a single-color Zerg wordmark, a single-color 'plz fix', and a full-color terminal-window graphic. Could you requote the White Vinyl Removable at 3 inches for 1,000 units split across the three?

Best,

Matthew

Hi Vicky,

That's right - 6 pieces total. Please put my Detroit address on the order as the buyer of record (Matt Eisner, 4727 Trumbull, Detroit, MI 48208), but still ship the second set to Idan Beck at 333 West San Carlos Street, San Jose, CA 95110.

Go ahead and draft the Alibaba order and I'll pay via Trade Assurance.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Cyan,

PDFs attached - they're vector. One note: the cover branding is single-color deboss or foil, not a CMYK color print, so the artwork is just the line art for the deboss/foil die - no color separations needed. The Zerg wordmark goes on the front cover; the terminal graphic is the optional back-cover graphic.

Are these enough to quote pricing at 300 and 500 units? If you need them re-laid-out with 3mm bleed and separate cutter/finishing layers once we lock the cover material, happy to provide that.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Threadbird team,

Following up on my note from earlier this week about a first run of Zerg team swag (tees, hats, hoodies) - wanted to check whether you've had a chance to pull together pricing and a rough lead time.

Happy to send over art files if that helps move things along.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Threadbird team,

Following up on my note from Monday about a first run of Zerg team swag (tees, hats, hoodies) - wanted to check whether you've had a chance to put together pricing and a rough lead time.

Happy to send over art files if that would help move things along.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Christan,

EPS attached. Shipping to Detroit - 4727 Trumbull, Detroit, MI 48208.

Given the case pack sizing, let's go to the next tier - could you send per-unit pricing at 96 with the single-color logo on the black hardcover Moleskines, plus the freight estimate to that address?

Best,

Matthew

Hi team,

Matt here, head of growth at Zerg Solutions (zergai.com). Saw the Revenue Technology Data Strategy & Operations Lead posting and figured it was worth a note.

Zerg Solutions runs that scope as a delivery engagement: AI-powered analytics, revenue tech, and data ops shipped by an embedded team rather than a single hire. We're modeled on the Vang Capital brief work we did with Algorand, Pento, and Quit Genius - scoped, time-bound, hands-on.

The OCC charter application looks like it expands the regulatory data and reporting surface meaningfully. We can stand up parallel capacity on the analytics and AI engineering side while the search runs, or own defined pieces of the scope on an ongoing basis.

Open to a quick call if useful.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Hermeus team,

Congrats on the $350M Series C and the plan to scale multiple airframes in parallel.

In our work with another venture-backed aerospace/defense company on a regulated cloud, the program, BOM, and ops software tends to fall behind the hardware once builds run in parallel. Zerg ships software for that gap, with agent integration into the engineering workflow.

No need for a call - happy to compare notes on where the internal-software gap is feeling tightest if useful.

Best,

Matthew

Hi team,

Saw the Series D close and the LC-14 build-out - the kind of ramp where program management, supply-chain/BOM, and manufacturing software tend to lag the hardware.

That's the surface area Zerg Solutions ships into: program-ops tooling with agent integration into the engineering workflow. Doing similar work now with a venture-backed aerospace/defense company.

Not looking to push a call - happy to compare notes if useful.

Best,

Matthew

Hi team,

Following up on the request I sent May 29 - the auto-reply said 3 business days and it's been 10. Trying to keep this moving ahead of the deed recording.

Property is 4727/4731 Trumbull St, Detroit on policy REIP0042566, and I need to change the named insured from Vang Advisory LLC to D4727 LLC. Could you walk me through the process? Ideally effective same-day as the deed records (no coverage gap), Fay Servicing staying on as mortgagee, no premium change expected.

Happy to send D4727's formation docs if useful.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Shannon,

Just following up on my note from May 29 re: the title transfer on 4727 Trumbull (closing order 7198557) - wanted to check what you need from me to get the deed prep moving on your side. Looping in Bristolle in case it routes better through her.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Matthew,

Liz passed along your name - I own a rental property at 4727 Trumbull in Detroit and recently had tenants leave behind some significant damage (the back porch/balcony in particular). I'd love to talk through my options for recovering damages and see if it's something you might be able to help with.

Would you have time for a quick intro call? I'm traveling starting Wednesday, so happy to grab time before then or once I'm back the following week - whatever works on your side.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Aaron,

I came across your firm while researching landlord-tenant attorneys in Detroit, and saw you handle collections of unpaid rents and damages for landlords.

I own a rental property at 4727 Trumbull, and recent former tenants left significant damage - the back porch/balcony in particular. I wanted to talk through options for recovering damages from them, and see if it's something your firm could help with.

Happy to do a quick intro call if you have time - flexible on timing, including next week.

Best,

Matthew

Hi team,

I'm Matt - I own a rental at 4727 Trumbull in Detroit and recently had tenants move out leaving significant damage, particularly to the back porch/balcony. Wanted to talk through options for recovering damages from them and whether your firm would be a fit given the landlord-tenant work you do.

Could we set up a quick intro call? Flexible on timing including next week.

Best,

Matthew

Hi team,

I own a rental property at 4727 Trumbull in Detroit and recently had tenants move out who left significant damage - the back porch/balcony in particular. I'd like to talk through options for recovering damages and whether your firm would be a fit to help.

Could we set up a quick intro call? Flexible on timing, including next week.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Matthew,

Wanted to make sure my earlier note reached you - resurfacing in case it got buried. Liz passed along your name re: a rental property I own at 4727 Trumbull in Detroit. Recent former tenants left some significant damage (back porch/balcony in particular), and I'd love to talk through options for recovering damages and see if it's something you might be able to help with.

Any chance you'd have time for a quick intro call? Flexible on timing - happy to grab something this week or next.

Best,

Matthew

Hi Matthew,

Liz passed along your name. I own a rental property at 4727 Trumbull in Detroit and recently had tenants leave behind some significant damage - the back porch/balcony in particular. I'd love to talk through options for recovering damages and see if it's something you might be able to help with.

Any chance you'd have time for a quick intro call this week or next? Flexible on timing.
